rulings.Case Title: VLADIMIR MIROV VS. ARKADY BERGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With Order Of The Court, For A Monetary Sanction, For A Sanction Payable To The Court, And For An Order To Show Cause Why Gary Varum Should Not Be Held In Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday July 10, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT ALEC BERGER'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Order Of The Court, For A Monetary Sanction, For A Sanction Payable To The Court, And For An Order To Show Cause Why Gary Varum Should Not Be Held In Contempt. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to July 22, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. to be heard by Judge Goldsmith on the Law and Motion calendar. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO GONZALEZ VS. DWAIN ALAN DAVIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendants Financial Position
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 10, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF FERNANDO GONZALEZ'S Motion For An Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendants Financial Position. The motion for an order permitting discovery of Defendant Dwain Alan Davis' financial position is granted pursuant to Civ. Code sec. 3295(c). Plaintiff has submitted sufficient evidence that there is a substantial probability that he will prevail on his claim for punitive damages. A hearing on this matter is required. The parties shall meet and confer on a protective order in advance of the hearing and have a proposed protective order to present to the Court at the hearing. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN CAMPOS et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER - et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Table Of Contents Of Exhibits; Declaration Of Lisa T. Ungerer; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 10, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANTS DAVID KNAPP, M.D., AND WILLIAM MILLER, M.D.'S Motion for Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendants met their initial burden establishing that Plaintiffs were not genuinely ignorant of the Defendants' identity at the time the complaint was filed. The complaint alleges specific facts as to the basis for the Doe defendants' liability - delay in ordering a CAT scan. (Complaint, par. 7.) Defendants submit evidence establishing that Plaintiffs were aware that Defendants were the doctors treating the decedent during the relevant time period. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts 1-30.) Thus, Defendants have met their initial burden establishing that Plaintiffs did not lack knowledge of Defendants' "connection with the case or with his injuries." (McOwen v. Grossman (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 937, 942.) Plaintiffs do not raise a triable issue of material fact in opposition. The evidence submitted does not address what Plaintiffs actually knew at the time the complaint was filed. Further, the Court finds this case to be analogous to Dover v. Sadowinski (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 113. As in Dover, the undisputed evidence establishes that Plaintiffs had actual and contemporaneous knowledge of the identities and roles played by Defendants in caring for the decedent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FELIX ESCOBAR VS. HERRERO BUILDERS INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 10, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT HERRERO BUILDERS INCORPORATED'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff was terminated because of his disability. At paragraphs 23 and 24 of his deposition, plaintiff declares that Tom Herrero (defendant's Superintendent of Logistics) expressly told plaintiff that he should no longer show up at the hospital worksite because plaintiff had work restrictions based on his disability. This is direct evidence of disability discrimination. Defendant objects to this evidence based on hearsay. The court overrules that objection because the evidence is an admissible admission. This evidence supports all of plaintiff's causes of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff was a qualified employee who could perform his job duties with a reasonable accommodation. "A highly fact-specific inquiry is necessary to determine what a particular job's essential functions are." Cripe v. City of San Jose (9th Cir. 2001) 261 F.3d 877, 888, fn. 12. Defendant admitted that plaintiff could perform many of his job duties with the accommodation. (Carroll Depo., 110:15-112:16). The court denies summary judgment/adjudication because there are triable issues of material fact as to all of plaintiff's causes of action. Thus, the motion is denied as to issues 1-9. The court denies summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages (issue 10). Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of production. Defendant does not cite any specific evidence regarding whether a managing agent, director, or officer of defendant acted maliciously. It is unclear whether Mr. Herrero qualifies as a managing agent of defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
